title: Imperialism
description: Test your knowledge of the American Age of Empire!

assessment_items.question_content:
What did historian **Frederick Jackson Turner** claim in 1893?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Turner did not specifically mention Manifest Destiny in his 1893 claim.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He claimed that the American frontier was ‘closed’; Americans could no longer be pioneers on the continent.
He claimed that it was time to start exploring the oceans surrounding the United States since Americans had already settled all of the land.
He stated that Manifest Destiny would always be the defining feature of the American mentality.

assessment_items.question_content:
What were some reasons the United States entered into the imperial game?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: After its victory in the Spanish-American War, the United States felt increasing pressure to assert itself as a world power.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: pressure to assert itself as a world power
access to raw materials and goods in potential colonies
desire to bring more immigrants from Asia and Latin America into the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
The explosion of the American ship **_USS Maine_** precipitated which war? 





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The _Maine_ exploded in the harbor of Havana, Cuba.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Spanish-American War
World War I
The Mexican-American War

assessment_items.question_content:
After they declared war on Spain in 1898, the US Congress passed the **Teller Amendment.** This stated that the US would help Cuba gain freedom from Spain but


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Cuba is not, nor has it ever been, the forty-sixth state in the Union.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States would not annex Cuba.
The United States would not interfere with any other Spanish colonial possessions, such as the Philippines.
The United States would annex Cuba as the forty-sixth state.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Rough Riders** were a volunteer cavalry regiment led by Teddy Roosevelt during the Spanish-American War. What was the main reason for their success?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: It was the **American** forces that were staggered by tropical illnesses.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They received support from African American soldiers serving in segregated cavalry units.
They were highly trained soldiers who had fought in the Civil War
Most of the opposing forces were ill with tropical diseases such as malaria and yellow fever

assessment_items.question_content:
One famous member of the **Anti-Imperialist League** was





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Kipling’s “white man’s burden” implies that white people have the responsibility to colonize and “protect” the lands of nonwhite people.  A collection of colonies beneath a central government is called an empire.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Andrew Carnegie, who didn’t want the US to govern far-away people.
Rudyard Kipling, who believed in the “white man’s burden.”
Josiah Strong, who did not believe it was the responsibility of whites to attempt to “civilize” other cultures

assessment_items.question_content:
What was Cuba’s role in the peace talks in the Treaty of Paris and the **Platt Amendment**?




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Cuba was not the leading nation in the Treaty of Paris.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cuba did not have a role in negotiating terms in the Treaty of Paris; Spain agreed to cede Cuba to the US. In the Platt Amendment, Cuba agreed to American intervention and to lease Guantánamo Bay to the US.
As one of the principal combatants, Cuba was the leading nation in the Treaty of Paris, dictating terms to Spain and the United States
Cuba, the Philippines, Puerto Rico, and Guam each sent one emissary to the treaty negotiations, while the United States and Spain sent dozens

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Roosevelt Corollary** was Theodore Roosevelt’s addition to the Monroe Doctrine; it stated that





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Communism was not yet a government that had successfully seized power when Roosevelt was president.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States would become involved in the economic affairs of Latin America whenever necessary to protect them from the influence of European powers.
The United States would aid any nation in resisting a communist insurgency
The United States expected its allies in Latin America to be responsible for their own defense, but would come to their defense when requested

assessment_items.question_content:
How did Roosevelt react to **Alfred Thayer Mahan’s** suggestion to build a canal for ships to pass through Central America?





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Rugged or not, it was not in the interest of America’s shipping business to take the long way around Cape Horn—a very dangerous passage at the southern tip of South America.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He supported the idea and his administration pushed forward with plans to build the Panama Canal.
He eschewed the idea, asserting that Americans’ ability to navigate around South America was a testament to “rugged individualism.”
He supported Mahan’s ideals in general but believed they were not feasible within the political climate of the time.

assessment_items.question_content:
The US government's reaction to American sugar plantation owners’ attempted coup in Hawaii in 1893 is indicative of





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Ostensibly, the United States entered the war with Spain to liberate Cuba, not to annex it.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: mixed feelings about entering into the imperialist game; the US government refused to cooperate with the coup.
strong American support for imperialism; the US government immediately annexed Hawaii and embarked on plans to annex the Philippines and Cuba.

widespread anti-imperialist sentiment throughout the United States, which strengthened the Anti-Imperialist League and prompted legislation outlawing any future territorial acquisitions outside of the US mainland.
